---
title: "R Notebook"
output: html_notebook
---

This is an [R Markdown](http://rmarkdown.rstudio.com) Notebook. When you execute code within the notebook, the results appear beneath the code. 

Try executing this chunk by clicking the *Run* button within the chunk or by placing your cursor inside it and pressing *Cmd+Shift+Enter*. 

```{r Libraries}
library("geomorph")
library("readr")
library("readxl")
library("ggplot2")
library("patchwork")
library("stringr")
library("dplyr")
library("RColorBrewer")
library("cowplot")
library("ggpubr")
```

```{r Background Theme}
# theme_black = function(base_size = 12, base_family = "") {
# 
#   theme_grey(base_size = base_size, base_family = base_family) %+replace%
# 
#     theme(
#       # Specify axis options
#       axis.line = element_blank(),
#       axis.text.x = element_text(size = base_size*0.8, color = "white", lineheight = 0.9),
#       axis.text.y = element_text(size = base_size*0.8, color = "white", lineheight = 0.9),
#       axis.ticks = element_line(color = "white", size  =  0.2),
#       axis.title.x = element_text(size = base_size, color = "white", margin = margin(0, 10, 0, 0)),
#       axis.title.y = element_text(size = base_size, color = "white", angle = 90, margin = margin(0, 10, 0, 0)),
#       axis.ticks.length = unit(0.3, "lines"),
#       # Specify legend options
#       legend.background = element_rect(color = NA, fill = "black"),
#       legend.key = element_rect(color = "black",  fill = "black"),
#       legend.key.size = unit(1.2, "lines"),
#       legend.key.height = NULL,
#       legend.key.width = NULL,
#       legend.text = element_text(size = base_size*0.8, color = "white"),
#       legend.title = element_text(size = base_size*0.8, face = "bold", hjust = 0, color = "white"),
#       legend.position = "right",
#       legend.text.align = NULL,
#       legend.title.align = NULL,
#       legend.direction = "vertical",
#       legend.box = NULL,
#       # Specify panel options
#       panel.background = element_rect(fill = "black", color  =  NA),
#       panel.border = element_rect(fill = NA, color = "white"),
#       panel.grid.major = element_blank(),
#       panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
#       panel.margin = unit(0.5, "lines"),
#       # Specify facetting options
#       strip.background = element_rect(fill = "grey30", color = "grey10"),
#       strip.text.x = element_text(size = base_size*0.8, color = "white"),
#       strip.text.y = element_text(size = base_size*0.8, color = "white",angle = -90),
#       # Specify plot options
#       plot.background = element_rect(color = "black", fill = "black"),
#       plot.title = element_text(size = base_size*1.2, color = "white"),
#       plot.margin = unit(rep(1, 4), "lines")
# 
#     )
# 
# }

```

```{r Reading in files/creating landmarks}
setwd("/Users/elynorein/Documents/GitHub/sandlanceverts/SL_Landmarks")
files <- as.data.frame(dir(pattern = ".csv", full.names = TRUE, ignore.case = TRUE))
names(files)[1] <- 'files'
files$files<- as.character(files$files)
samples = files$files
test = read.csv(files$files[1])

# read in landmark data
l = nrow(test) # number of landmarks
d = 3 # number of dimensions (2D or 3D)
s = length(samples) # number of specimens

LMs = array(dim=c(l, d, s)) 
lables = test$label
vert_label <- as.factor(sub(".*_(Vert\\d+)(?:_Landmarks)?\\.csv$", "\\1", files$files)) #define some groups for plotting

# Read in each file and stack into LM array
for (i in 1:s) {
  data <- read.csv(files$files[i], stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
  print(paste("Processing file:", files$files[i]))
  print(dim(data))
  print(colnames(data)[4:6])
  print(head(data[, 4:6]))
  marks <- as.array(as.matrix(data[, 4:6]))
  print(dim(marks))
  LMs[,,i] <- marks
}

# Label the rows with the file names
dimnames(LMs) = list(lables, c("x", "y", "z"), samples)

VertebraeLandmarkData <- read_csv("/Users/elynorein/Documents/GitHub/sandlanceverts/SL_Landmarks/VertebraeLandmarkData/VertebraeLandmarkData.csv")
files$FileName <- files$file
merged <- merge(files, VertebraeLandmarkData, by = "FileName", all.x = TRUE)

# Links
VertLinks <- read.csv("/Users/elynorein/Documents/GitHub/sandlanceverts/SL_Landmarks/VertLinks/VertLinks.csv", header = FALSE)
```

```{r Procrustes Superimposition/PCA}
# run Procrustes Superimposition
gpa <- gpagen(LMs)

plotAllSpecimens(gpa$coords, links = VertLinks, mean = TRUE)

plotOutliers(gpa$coords, inspect.outliers = T)

PCA <- gm.prcomp(gpa$coords)
cex_values <- scales::rescale(merged$SL, to = c(0.5, 2))  # from the 'scales' package
plot(PCA, pch = 21, bg = vert_label, cex = cex_values)
legend("topleft", pch = 22, pt.bg = unique(vert_label), legend = unique(vert_label), horiz = FALSE)

# Extract percent variance explained
percent_var <- (PCA$sdev^2) / sum(PCA$sdev^2) * 100

pc1_var <- round(percent_var[1], 1)
pc2_var <- round(percent_var[2], 1)

pc_scores <- PCA$x

plot(pc_scores[,1], pc_scores[,2],
     pch = 21, bg = vert_label, cex = cex_values,
     xlab = paste0("PC1 (", pc1_var, "%)"),
     ylab = paste0("PC2 (", pc2_var, "%)"))
legend("topleft", pch = 22, pt.bg = unique(vert_label), legend = unique(vert_label), horiz = FALSE)


pc1_var <- round(percent_var[2], 1)
pc2_var <- round(percent_var[3], 1)

plot(pc_scores[,2], pc_scores[,3],
     pch = 21, bg = vert_label, cex = cex_values,
     xlab = paste0("PC2 (", pc1_var, "%)"),
     ylab = paste0("PC3 (", pc2_var, "%)"))
legend("topleft", pch = 22, pt.bg = unique(vert_label), legend = unique(vert_label), horiz = FALSE)

```

```{r Mean Shape/Extreme Shapes}
# Get mean shape and shape at max PC1
ref <- mshape(gpa$coords)
target <- PCA$shapes$shapes.comp1$max  # or use $min for the other direction
# Calculate displacement vectors per landmark
pc1vec <- target - ref  # array: [landmark, dim]
# Compute magnitude of displacement (Euclidean distance) per landmark
disp <- sqrt(rowSums(pc1vec^2))

# Display how much each landmark contributes to the variation in PC1
barplot(disp, main = "Landmark Contributions to PC1",
        xlab = "Landmark", ylab = "Displacement Magnitude")

mag <- 3# scaling factor, increase to exaggerate displacement
target_scaled <- ref + (target - ref) * mag

# Project and plot onto XY
# Combine both sets of coordinates to get limits
x_range <- range(c(ref[,1], target_scaled[,1]))
y_range <- range(c(ref[,2], target_scaled[,2]))
plot(ref[,1], ref[,2], asp = 1, pch = 21, bg = "gray", 
     main = "PC1 Shape Change: XY Projection",
     xlab = "X", ylab = "Y", xlim = x_range, ylim = y_range)
arrows(ref[,1], ref[,2], target_scaled[,1], target_scaled[,2], 
       col = "black", lwd = 2, length = 0.1)
points(target_scaled[,1], target_scaled[,2], pch = 21, bg = "black")
#legend("topleft", legend = names(group_colors), fill = group_colors, border = "black", cex = 0.5,  bty = "n")
text(ref[,1], ref[,2], labels = 1:nrow(ref), pos = 3, cex = 0.7)

# Project and plot onto XZ
x_range <- range(c(ref[,1], target_scaled[,1]))
y_range <- range(c(ref[,3], target_scaled[,3]))
plot(ref[,1], ref[,3], asp = 1, pch = 21, bg = "gray",
     main = "PC1 Shape Change: XZ Projection",
     xlab = "X", ylab = "Z", xlim = x_range, ylim = y_range)
arrows(ref[,1], ref[,3],
       target_scaled[,1], target_scaled[,3],
       col = "black", lwd = 2, length = 0.1)
points(target_scaled[,1], target_scaled[,3], pch = 21, bg = "black")
#legend("topleft", legend = names(group_colors), fill = group_colors, border = "black", cex = 0.5,  bty = "n")
text(ref[,1], ref[,3], labels = 1:nrow(ref), pos = 3, cex = 0.7)

# Project and plot onto YZ
x_range <- range(c(ref[,2], target_scaled[,2]))
y_range <- range(c(ref[,3], target_scaled[,3]))
plot(ref[,2], ref[,3], asp = 1, pch = 21, bg = "gray",
     main = "PC1 Shape Change: YZ Projection",
     xlab = "Y", ylab = "Z", xlim = x_range, ylim = y_range)
arrows(ref[,2], ref[,3], target_scaled[,2], target_scaled[,3], 
       col = "black", lwd = 2, length = 0.1)
points(target[,2], target[,3], pch = 21, bg = "black")
text(ref[,2], ref[,3], labels = 1:nrow(ref), pos = 3, cex = 0.7)
```

```{r Landmark Contributions}
#####Extract Weights
# PCA$sdev contains the standard deviations for each PC
sdev <- PCA$sdev
# Compute variance (eigenvalues)
variance <- sdev^2
# Proportion of variance explained by each PC
prop_var <- variance / sum(variance)
# Percent variance explained
percent_var <- prop_var * 100
#print(percent_var)

# Number of landmarks
n_landmarks <- dim(PCA$rotation)[1] / 3
# Get PC loadings for each axis (x, y, z) per landmark
PC_loadings <- PCA$rotation  # matrix with rows = coords (X1, Y1, Z1, X2, ...) and columns = PCs
# Initialize matrix to store contributions per landmark
landmark_contributions <- data.frame(Landmark = 1:n_landmarks)

# Loop over first few PCs (say, first 3)
for (pc in 1:15) {
  # Extract loadings for PC `pc`, grouped by 3 rows per landmark
  pc_vector <- PC_loadings[, pc]
  
  # Sum squared loadings for X, Y, Z of each landmark
  contribution <- rowSums(matrix(pc_vector, ncol = 3, byrow = TRUE)^2)
  pc_label <- paste0("PC", pc, "_", round(percent_var[pc], 1), "%")
  # Add to dataframe
  landmark_contributions[[pc_label]] <- contribution
}
landmark_contributions$Label <- test$label
```

```{r Shape Predictions}

#Shape Predictions
PC <- PCA$x[,1]
preds <- shape.predictor(gpa$coords, x= PC, Intercept = FALSE,
                         pred1 = min(PC),
                         pred2 = max(PC)) # PC 1 extremes, more technically
par(mfrow=c(1, 2))
plotRefToTarget(ref, preds$pred1, links = VertLinks)
mtext("PC1 - Min.")
plotRefToTarget(ref, preds$pred2, links = VertLinks)
mtext("PC1 - Max.")


#Shape Predictions
PC <- PCA$x[,2]
preds <- shape.predictor(gpa$coords, x= PC, Intercept = FALSE, 
                         pred1 = min(PC),  
                         pred2 = max(PC)) # PC 1 extremes, more technically
par(mfrow=c(1, 2))
plotRefToTarget(ref, preds$pred1, links = VertLinks)
mtext("PC2 - Min.")
plotRefToTarget(ref, preds$pred2, links = VertLinks)
mtext("PC2 - Max.")

#Shape Predictions
PC <- PCA$x[,3]
preds <- shape.predictor(gpa$coords, x= PC, Intercept = FALSE, 
                         pred1 = min(PC),  
                         pred2 = max(PC)) # PC 1 extremes, more technically
par(mfrow=c(1, 2))
plotRefToTarget(ref, preds$pred1, links = VertLinks)
mtext("PC3 - Min.")
plotRefToTarget(ref, preds$pred2, links = VertLinks)
mtext("PC3 - Max.")
```

```{r save PC3 min and max as pictures}
png("/Users/elynorein/Documents/GitHub/sandlanceverts/SL_Landmarks/PC3_Min.png", width = 5, height = 5, units = "in", res = 600)
plotRefToTarget(ref, preds$pred1, links = VertLinks)
mtext("PC3 - Min.")
dev.off()

png("/Users/elynorein/Documents/GitHub/sandlanceverts/SL_Landmarks/PC3_Max.png", width = 5, height = 5, units = "in", res = 600)
plotRefToTarget(ref, preds$pred2, links = VertLinks)
mtext("PC3 - Max.")
dev.off()
```

```{r inverse color plots}
# Links
VertLinks <- read.csv("/Users/elynorein/Documents/GitHub/sandlanceverts/SL_Landmarks/VertLinks/VertLinks.csv", header = FALSE)

# Set black background globally
par(bg = "black", col.axis = "white", col.lab = "white", col.main = "white", col.sub = "white", fg = "white")

#Shape Predictions - PC1
PC <- PCA$x[,1]
preds <- shape.predictor(gpa$coords, x = PC, Intercept = FALSE,
                         pred1 = min(PC),
                         pred2 = max(PC))
par(mfrow = c(1, 2), bg = "black", fg = "white")
plotRefToTarget(ref, preds$pred1, links = VertLinks,
                gridPars = gridPar(pt.bg = "white", pt.size = 1.5,
                                   link.col = "white", tar.link.col = "white",
                                   grid.col = "white", tar.pt.bg = "white"))
mtext("PC1 - Min.", col = "white")
plotRefToTarget(ref, preds$pred2, links = VertLinks,
                gridPars = gridPar(pt.bg = "white", pt.size = 1.5,
                                   link.col = "white", tar.link.col = "white",
                                   grid.col = "white", tar.pt.bg = "white"))
mtext("PC1 - Max.", col = "white")

#Shape Predictions - PC2
PC <- PCA$x[,2]
preds <- shape.predictor(gpa$coords, x = PC, Intercept = FALSE,
                         pred1 = min(PC),
                         pred2 = max(PC))
par(mfrow = c(1, 2), bg = "black", fg = "white")
plotRefToTarget(ref, preds$pred1, links = VertLinks,
                gridPars = gridPar(pt.bg = "white", pt.size = 1.5,
                                   link.col = "white", tar.link.col = "white",
                                   grid.col = "white", tar.pt.bg = "white"))
mtext("PC2 - Min.", col = "white")
plotRefToTarget(ref, preds$pred2, links = VertLinks,
                gridPars = gridPar(pt.bg = "white", pt.size = 1.5,
                                   link.col = "white", tar.link.col = "white",
                                   grid.col = "white", tar.pt.bg = "white"))
mtext("PC2 - Max.", col = "white")

#Shape Predictions - PC3
PC <- PCA$x[,3]
preds <- shape.predictor(gpa$coords, x = PC, Intercept = FALSE,
                         pred1 = min(PC),
                         pred2 = max(PC))
# Saving PC3 Extremes
png("/Users/elynorein/Documents/GitHub/sandlanceverts/SL_Landmarks/PC3_Min.png", width = 800, height = 600, bg = "white")
par(bg = "white", fg = "black")
plotRefToTarget(ref, preds$pred1, links = VertLinks,
                gridPars = gridPar(pt.bg = "black", pt.size = 1.5,
                                   link.col = "black", tar.link.col = "black",
                                   grid.col = "black", tar.pt.bg = "black"))
mtext("PC3 - Min.", col = "black")
dev.off()

png("/Users/elynorein/Documents/GitHub/sandlanceverts/SL_Landmarks/PC3_Max.png", width = 800, height = 600, bg = "white")
par(bg = "white", fg = "black")
plotRefToTarget(ref, preds$pred2, links = VertLinks,
                gridPars = gridPar(pt.bg = "black", pt.size = 1.5,
                                   link.col = "black", tar.link.col = "black",
                                   grid.col = "black", tar.pt.bg = "black"))
mtext("PC3 - Max.", col = "black")
dev.off()
```

```{r PCA Plots}
pca_df <- data.frame(
  PC1 = pc_scores[, 1],
  PC2 = pc_scores[, 2],
  PC3 = pc_scores[, 3],
  vert_label = vert_label,
  SL = merged$SL,
  cex_values = cex_values
)

vert_colors <- c("#E69F00", "#F0E442", "#56B4E9", "#0072B2", "#9955BB")

PC1v2plot <- ggplot(pca_df, aes(x = PC1, y = PC2, fill = vert_label, size = cex_values)) +
  geom_point(shape = 21, color = "black", alpha = 0.8) +
  scale_size_continuous(
    range = c(1, 6),
    name = "SL",
    labels = c("33mm", "58mm", "75mm", "95mm")
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(                                        
    values = vert_colors,
    name = "Vertebra",
    labels = c("0% VL", "25% VL", "50% VL", "75% VL", "100% VL")
  ) +
  guides(
    fill = guide_legend(override.aes = list(size = 5), nrow = 1),  # <-- nrow = 1 makes it horizontal
    size = guide_legend(nrow = 1)                                   # <-- same for size legend
  ) +
  labs(
    x = paste0("PC1 (", round(percent_var[1], 1), "%)"),
    y = paste0("PC2 (", round(percent_var[2], 1), "%)"),
  ) +
  theme_classic() +
  theme(
    axis.title = element_text(size = 8),
    axis.text  = element_text(size = 7)
  ) + 
  coord_fixed(ratio = 1.25)

PC2v3plot <- ggplot(pca_df, aes(x = PC2, y = PC3, fill = vert_label, size = cex_values)) +
  geom_point(shape = 21, color = "black", alpha = 0.8) +
  scale_size_continuous(
    range = c(1, 6),
    name = "SL",
    labels = c("33mm", "58mm", "75mm", "95mm")
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(                                         
    values = vert_colors,
    name = "Vertebra",
    labels = c("0% VL", "25% VL", "50% VL", "75% VL", "100% VL")
  ) +
  guides(
    fill = guide_legend(override.aes = list(size = 5), nrow = 1),
    size = guide_legend(nrow = 1)
  ) +
  labs(
    x = paste0("PC2 (", round(percent_var[2], 1), "%)"),
    y = paste0("PC3 (", round(percent_var[3], 1), "%)"),
  ) +
  theme_classic() +
  theme(
    axis.title = element_text(size = 8),
    axis.text  = element_text(size = 7)
  ) + 
  coord_fixed(ratio = 1.25)

combined_plot <- PC1v2plot / PC2v3plot + 
  plot_layout(guides = "collect") &        # & applies to all plots
  theme(
    legend.position = "bottom",            # <-- moves legend below
    legend.box = "vertical"               # <-- stacks the two legends (fill + size)
  )

ggsave("GMPCAPlotsCombined.png", plot = combined_plot, width = 6, height = 7.5, units = "in")
```
